key_insight: This protocol tests whether a resilience-focused, virtual physical activity program can help adolescents with chronic musculoskeletal pain stay active despite pain.
clinical_relevance: If feasible and acceptable, the intervention could support better pain-related functioning and physical activity engagement in adolescents with chronic pain.
method: Single-site, pilot phase 2, single-group non-randomized trial in 40 adolescents aged 12–17 years.
Four virtual intervention sessions targeting self-efficacy, motivation, adaptive coping, and mental flexibility.
Baseline, post-intervention, and 3-month follow-up measures include questionnaires, physical functioning tests, and activity monitoring.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a feasibility-focused pilot design rather than efficacy results, with recruitment, retention, attendance, fidelity, and measure completion as primary outcomes.
watch_item: Because this is a small, single-group pilot without randomization or a control arm, any changes in activity or pain outcomes will be preliminary and may not generalize widely.
paper_title: Resilience factors, pain, and physical activity in adolescent chronic musculoskeletal pain: design and protocol of a pilot phase 2 single-group, non-randomized clinical trial
